description.task: Implement an LRU (Least Recently Used) cache with O(1) get and put operations. Use a HashMap for O(1) lookup combined with a doubly-linked list to maintain access order. When capacity is exceeded, evict the least recently used entry.
instructions: After put(1),put(2): order=[2,1]; get(1) makes order=[1,2]; put(3) evicts 2 → order=[3,1]

description.task: Solve classic hash-based problems: (1) Two Sum — find indices of two numbers that add to target; (2) Group Anagrams — group strings that are anagrams of each other; (3) Subarray Sum Equals K — count subarrays with sum equal to k.
instructions: twoSum: if(seen.containsKey(complement)) return new int[]{seen.get(complement),i}; seen.put(nums[i],i);
GroupAnagrams: [ate,eat,tea],[bat],[nat,tan]; SubarraySum [1,1,1] k=2: subarrays [1,1] at positions 0-1 and 1-2 = 2
description.expectedOutput:
TwoSum [2,7,11,15] t=9: [0, 1]
TwoSum [3,2,4] t=6:      [1, 2]
GroupAnagrams: [[ate, eat, tea], [bat], [nat, tan]]
SubarraySum [1,1,1] k=2: 2

description.task: Simulate a simple database index using a HashMap<String, List<Integer>> where keys are column values and values are row IDs. Support insert, query (find all rows matching a value), and count distinct values (cardinality).
instructions: Eng employees have row IDs 0, 2, 3; HR → 1, 4
Alice is only in row 0; name cardinality = 5 (all unique); dept cardinality = 2 (Eng, HR)
The cardinality method uses stream filter — already implemented
This pattern mirrors how database engines build hash indexes for O(1) lookups
description.expectedOutput:
Eng rows: [0, 2, 3]
HR rows:  [1, 4]
Alice rows: [0]
Name cardinality: 5
Dept cardinality: 2
